label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane booked a flight for tomorrow, which is July 29, 2002. Wait, hold on. If today is the day she's booking, then "tomorrow" would be the 29th. So the flight is on the 29th. Now the question is asking for the date 24 hours later. So adding 24 hours to July 29, 2002. Hmm. Let me think. A day has 24 hours, right? So 24 hours from any given time would be exactly one day later. But wait, does adding 24 hours always equal exactly a day later? Well, in most cases yes, but sometimes when you cross over into a different month or there's daylight saving time changes, but the problem doesn't mention anything about time zones or daylight saving, so I think we can ignore that here. So if the flight is on July 29, 2002, then 24 hours later would be July 30, 2002. Let me check the months. July has 31 days, so the 29th plus one day is the 30th. Therefore, the answer should be 07/30/2002. Let me look at the options again. Option C is 07/30/2002. So that's choice C. Wait, but wait a second. Sometimes people get confused between adding a day versus adding 24 hours. For example, if you have a flight departing at 10 AM on the 29th, then 24 hours later would be 10 AM on the 30th. But if you add a day, it's still the same. So in this case,
